g.title: First Order Linear Recurrence Relations

text.data-narration: A first order linear recurrence relation is an equation of the form: F(n) = a \* F(n-1) + b \* F(n-2) + ... + c \* F(n-k) where F(n) is the value of the function at n, a, b, ..., c, and k are constants, and n is a positive integer. The given recurrence relation is said to be linear because it involves only addition and multiplication of the function values, and not any higher-order operations such as exponentiation or root extraction.

g.g:
<text x="20" y="270" font-size="18" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" fill="#333333" data-narration="There are two types of first order linear recurrence relations:
- Homogeneous recurrence relations: F(n) = a \* F(n-1) + b \* F(n-2)
- Non-homogeneous recurrence relations: F(n) = a \* F(n-1) + b \* F(n-2) + c"/>

text.data-narration: Euler's method is a technique used to solve non-homogeneous first order linear recurrence relations. The general solution of a non-homogeneous recurrence relation is given by: F(n) = r1 \* F(n-1) + r2 \* F(n-2) + ... + rk where r1, r2, ..., rk are constants. A particular solution of a non-homogeneous recurrence relation is given by: Fp(n) = A \* n + B where A and B are constants. The general solution of a non-homogeneous recurrence relation is given by: F(n) = Fh(n) + Fp(n) where Fh(n) is the homogeneous solution and Fp(n) is the particular solution.

foreignObject.data-narration: F(n) = Fh(n) + Fp(n) where Fh(n) is the homogeneous solution and Fp(n) is the particular solution.
